description_long: This object is a compact, rectangular remote control device with a slightly curved bottom edge and a flat top surface. The top face features a small display screen, a prominent circular power button, and several smaller round buttons arranged in a functional layout. The surface is predominantly light gray or white, with black printed text and a distinctive wolf logo labeled 'DECK' near the bottom. The buttons are raised and tactile, likely made of plastic, and the device appears to be constructed from a hard plastic shell. The back and sides are smooth, with a visible infrared emitter or sensor port at one end, suggesting it is used to wirelessly control another electronic device. The overall design is ergonomic, fitting comfortably in one hand, and is typical of remote controls used for appliances or entertainment systems.
description: A compact, light-colored remote control with a display, tactile buttons, and a wolf logo labeled 'DECK' on the front. It features a smooth plastic body and an infrared port for wireless operation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the display, main power button, and all control buttons, with the 'DECK' logo clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the slim profile and the raised buttons on the top surface.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the infrared port and the back of the device, with the display and buttons partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and button arrangement, with the logo visible at the far end.
